title: Author PROP — workspace-sync universal-pusher destructive overwrite of append-only .jsonl files (data-loss vector confirmed 2026-05-30T09:11Z)
background:
Tinker FND-02 from run 2026-05-31T02-40 surfaced a confirmed data-loss event: workspace-sync commit `3c62327` at 2026-05-30T09:11:20Z PUSHED FUSE's stale base of `monitor/tinker/queue-history.jsonl` over git, deleting the previous tinker run's row that had been written via the clone-and-push path. The deleted row was tinker's audit-trail metric for PROP-030 backlog-trend tracking; reconstructing it required re-running the metric computation.

Root cause: Universal-pusher mode (2026-04-26) lets workspace-sync push FUSE→git for runtime data files (data/, decisions/, applied-patches/, queue-history.jsonl, etc.) when FUSE has newer mtime. The smart_copy helper does mtime + content-hash guards but operates on a per-FILE basis — it doesn't model append-only semantics. So when:
  (1) Tinker writes a new row to queue-history.jsonl via clone-and-push (git side now has N+1 rows)
  (2) Some local agent or workspace-sync's previous cycle updates the FUSE-side queue-history.jsonl mtime without picking up the git-side row (e.g., touched via a different write path that bypassed the FUSE update)
  (3) workspace-sync's next cycle sees FUSE-mtime > last-git-commit-time and triggers a push
the push overwrites git's N+1 rows with FUSE's N rows. The N+1-th row is silently lost.

Same risk pattern affects:
  - monitor/decisions/closure-ledger.jsonl (decider's closure audit trail)
  - monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl (direction-guard skip log)
  - monitor/sloppytoppy/calibration-audits.jsonl (PROP-041 Phase 2 post-hoc audit)
  - any future .jsonl in monitor/ classified as append-only-but-multi-writer.

This is functionally a regression of the 2026-05-21 workspace-sync mass-delete pattern, scoped to .jsonl files. PROP-051's A1-A4 safety gates catch large-blast-radius deletes (>50 files), but a single .jsonl-row loss flies under that threshold.

evidence.the_deletion_commit: 3c62327 at 2026-05-30T09:11:20Z — 'workspace-sync: FUSE→git sync 2026-05-30T09:11:20Z'. Inspect via `gh api repos/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/commits/3c62327 | jq '.files[]|select(.filename=="monitor/tinker/queue-history.jsonl")'` to see the deletion in the patch.
evidence.tinker_recovery_evidence: Tinker 2026-05-31T02-40 FND-02 detected the missing row and appended a recovery row. The recovery row's `recovery_note` field carries the diagnosis.
evidence.writer_pattern_clone_vs_FUSE: Tinker and decider write append-only .jsonl rows via clone-and-push (git is source of truth). workspace-sync's universal-pusher assumes FUSE is source of truth when mtime > git-last-commit. The two assumptions collide for files written via the clone-and-push pattern.
evidence.scope_of_risk: Any .jsonl file under monitor/ that is written by an agent via clone-and-push AND has its FUSE-mtime updated by ANY other write path (workspace-sync itself, another scheduled agent, a FUSE-side sync) is vulnerable to the same overwrite. The mtime guard alone is insufficient because mtime doesn't encode 'has this version of the file seen all the rows that exist on git'.

requested_work: Step 1 — Confirm the diagnosis: read smart_copy in workspace-sync.md and trace the per-file decision tree for .jsonl files. Confirm whether mtime-only is the trigger (which would match FND-02's observed pattern), or whether there's a hash-comparison path that should have caught the regression.

Step 2 — Enumerate the affected .jsonl files: walk monitor/ for .jsonl files, classify each as (a) append-only single-writer (low risk), (b) append-only multi-writer (high risk — the FND-02 class), (c) snapshot-replaced (not append-only, not affected). queue-history.jsonl, closure-ledger.jsonl, workspace-sync-skips.jsonl, calibration-audits.jsonl are the known class-b candidates.

Step 3 — Propose the fix: design a smart_copy variant for .jsonl files in class b. Options to evaluate: (i) MERGE instead of REPLACE — for .jsonl, the smart_copy reads BOTH versions, takes the UNION of rows by some idempotency key (e.g., timestamp + run_id), and writes the merged version. Risk: merge logic adds complexity and possible duplicate-row bugs. (ii) REJECT pushes from FUSE for class-b .jsonl entirely — only the writer's clone-and-push path is authoritative; FUSE never pushes these. Risk: if a writer fails mid-write and leaves FUSE-newer-than-git, the row is lost — but at least no overwrite. (iii) DEFENSIVE READ before push — workspace-sync's universal-pusher does a `git pull --rebase` immediately before pushing each .jsonl, so any concurrent rows added since last sync are pulled in. Risk: rebase conflicts on .jsonl edge cases. (iv) Mark .jsonl files explicitly in build.js OWNERSHIP as `git-append-only` and have workspace-sync refuse to push them. Cleanest option; aligns with the existing OWNERSHIP table pattern.

Step 4 — Recommend ONE option and author PROP-065 (or amendment to PROP-051). Include: file-list scope (enumerated from Step 2), exact smart_copy patch (per option), idempotency contract, edge cases (what if the writer's clone has uncommitted rows on disk when workspace-sync evaluates? what about race with PROP-061/064 git→FUSE sync?), verification path (synthetic test: write a row via clone-and-push, then touch FUSE-mtime, then run workspace-sync, then verify the clone row survives).

Step 5 — Audit recovery: enumerate all .jsonl files for missing rows. queue-history.jsonl is the known case; check the other 3 (closure-ledger, workspace-sync-skips, calibration-audits) for any rows that should exist (based on agent run reports) but don't. If found, document the gaps in a recovery summary.
non_goals: Do NOT modify workspace-sync's FUSE-only pushes for non-jsonl files — that direction is working correctly and is the rescue path for decider 403 push failures.
Do NOT extend smart_copy to write-side ordering guarantees beyond mtime + hash + the new append-only-aware path — out of scope.

Do NOT touch monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl as a writer-side fix — workspace-sync writes that one itself and the existing logic already handles its write path correctly. Just confirm the read-side path doesn't trigger a destructive overwrite.

context_pointers.smart_copy_implementation: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md — search for 'smart_copy' to find the helper definition and per-file dispatch logic
context_pointers.ownership_table: build.js — the OWNERSHIP object (per CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules). Adding a 'git-append-only' category here for option (iv) is the smallest-blast-radius design.

expected_output: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-065.json (or PROP-051-amendment-NNN.json) with: option chosen + rationale, smart_copy patch, file-list scope, idempotency contract, edge cases, verification path, optional Step 5 recovery summary.
estimated_effort: Mode 4 PROP-authoring + Mode 1 audit. ~30-40 min Opus.
priority_note: HIGH because the .jsonl files affected are forensic gold (closure-ledger feeds revert scripts, queue-history feeds PROP-030 backlog trend, etc). Silent row-loss is the worst kind of data-loss bug — it doesn't trigger alarms and the missing row is only detected by tinker's audit-trail check on a later run. Each day this stays unfixed risks another silent loss event.
